summary: The S2000 is exceptionally reliable for a high-revving sports car. The F20C engine is nearly bulletproof when maintained. Issues are minor and age-related rather than design flaws.

issues.name: Soft Top Wear
issues.description: The cloth convertible top deteriorates with age and UV exposure. The rear plastic window yellows and cracks, reducing visibility.

issues.name: Snap Oversteer (AP1)
issues.description: Not a reliability issue but a handling characteristic — the AP1's short wheelbase and aggressive rear toe settings can cause sudden oversteer when lifting off throttle mid-corner.

issues.name: Timing Chain Tensioner Noise
issues.description: The timing chain tensioner can develop a rattle on cold start. Rarely leads to failure but should be inspected on high-mileage cars.

issues.name: Clutch Master Cylinder Failure
issues.description: The clutch master cylinder can develop internal leaks, causing soft or spongy clutch pedal feel and difficulty shifting.

issues.name: Differential Whine
issues.description: The rear differential can develop a whine at highway speeds on higher-mileage cars. The Torsen LSD wears over time with spirited driving.

buyingTips: The F20C engine is incredibly durable — look for consistent oil changes with Honda-spec or equivalent oil
Inspect the soft top carefully — replacement is expensive and a yellowed rear window is the first sign of age
Check for accident history — the S2000 is commonly wrecked by inexperienced drivers. Get a thorough frame inspection
AP1 vs AP2 — the AP2 (2004+) has a detuned engine but improved handling and interior. Both are excellent
Low-mileage examples command premium prices — this car is only appreciating in value
